evals.expected_output: Runs standard mode scrape without asking for mode, returns page-typed markdown with summary.
expectations: Defaults to standard mode without asking clarifying questions
Runs firecrawl_scrape.py with --domain acme.com --mode standard
Map runs first (1 credit) to discover site URLs
Classifies URLs into page types using PAGE_PATTERNS
Scrapes only the best-match URL per page type (not all URLs)
JSON scan saved to runs/<run-id>/scans/acme.com.json
Tracker updated with status, credits used, pages scraped
Shows summary: pages found, pages scraped, credits used, status
Offers to write results to Google Sheet

evals.type: Type 2 - Single domain, extract mode with cost warning
evals.expected_output: Uses extract mode with default schema, warns about ~20+ credit cost before proceeding.
expectations: Recognizes this as extract mode
Warns that extract mode costs ~20+ credits (not 1) before proceeding
Waits for user confirmation before running
Uses default schema: founder, headcount_clues, tech_mentions, funding_clues, product_category, customers_mentioned, partners, investors, year_founded, locations
Runs firecrawl_scrape.py with --mode extract
Returns structured JSON with extracted fields
Offers custom schema option if user wants different fields

evals.prompt: Scrape these 60 domains from domains.txt in standard mode
evals.expected_output: Detects batch >50, shows estimated credits and cost, asks confirmation before running.
expectations: Detects batch size >50 domains
Estimates total credits: 60 x ~6 = ~360 credits
Calculates estimated cost: 360 x $0.001 = $0.36 (Standard plan)
Shows estimate and asks for confirmation before proceeding
Runs with --batch domains.txt --mode standard
Tracker handles partial failures gracefully
Shows progress during batch processing
Final summary shows completed/failed/skipped counts and total credits

evals.expected_output: Reads existing tracker, skips completed domains, processes only remaining ones.
expectations: Runs with --resume runs/2026-07-08-batch
Reads existing tracker.json to identify completed domains
Skips already-completed domains (no duplicate credit spend)
Only spends credits on remaining/failed domains
Updates tracker incrementally as each domain completes
Shows final summary with total completed across both sessions

evals.prompt: Get everything you can from beta.io
evals.expected_output: Recognizes ambiguity between deep and extract, asks user to pick a mode.
expectations: Does NOT default to standard - 'everything' signals more than default
Identifies two possible interpretations: deep (~11 credits, more page types) vs extract (~20+ credits, structured LLM extraction)
Explains the difference between deep and extract modes
Asks user which mode they want before running
Runs only after user confirms mode choice

evals.prompt: Scrape example.com and write results to this sheet: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/ABC123/edit
evals.expected_output: Scrapes domain, then writes results to the specified Google Sheet using sheets_writer.py.
expectations: Extracts spreadsheet ID ABC123 from URL
Runs firecrawl_scrape.py first with --domain example.com --mode standard
After scrape completes, runs sheets_writer.py with --run-dir and --spreadsheet-id ABC123
Sheet columns match spec: Domain, Status, Mode, Date, URLs Found, Pages Scraped, Credits Used, then page content columns
Does NOT use Google Sheets MCP for writing (fails on large content)

evals.prompt: Scrape 600 domains from big-list.txt in deep mode
evals.expected_output: Estimates cost over $10, triggers hard stop requiring explicit user approval.
expectations: Detects batch size >500 domains
Suggests switching to minimal mode to reduce cost
Estimates credits: 600 x ~11 (deep) = ~6,600 credits
Calculates cost: 6,600 x $0.001 = $6.60 or notes it could be higher with stealth proxy
Requires explicit user approval before proceeding
Does NOT start scraping until user confirms
